//: The example plugin's frontend, packaged as a real plugin package so a host can compose it by name via the plugin manifest (see octarq's web/octarq.plugins.json — the OSS default edition ships this). Consumed as source: the host's Vite build bundles ./index.ts directly, resolving react + @octarq/plugin-sdk from the host (peer deps, single instance). Differently-scoped (@acme) on purpose: it demonstrates a third-party plugin.
name: @acme/octarq-plugin-hello
